revision.text:
'''Shidori Shrine''' (倭文神社) refers to Shinto shrines bearing the name "Shidori" (also read as Shizuri, Shitori, or Shidori).
These shrines enshrine '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto}}''' (also known as '''Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto''', '''Ameno Hachio''', or '''Takehatsuchi''') as the '''deity of weaving''', alongside '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神}}''' (also known as '''Amano Yachichihime''' or '''Amanoorihime'''). These shrines were established by the '''{{ill|Shidori clan|ja|倭文氏}}''', who worshipped Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto as their ancestral deity.
The '''origin of these shrines is attributed to''' the '''{{ill|Katsuraki Shitori ni Imasu Ame no Ha Ikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社}}''' in '''Katsuragi, Nara Prefecture'''. However, while the introduction and promotion of silk weaving technology in Japan is credited to '''{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}''', some Shidori Shrines claim their origins date back to the reign of '''{{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}'''. Therefore, these shrines provide '''important historical evidence''' regarding '''when and where silk weaving developed in Japan (Wa).'''
Additionally, in the '''2016 animated film "Your Name"''', the fictional '''{{ill|Miyamizu Shrine|ja|宮水神社}}''' is depicted as enshrining '''Shidori deity Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto'''.
== Major Shidori Shrines ==
The following shrines are listed in the '''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}''':
* '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Nanba District, Kōzuke Province''' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)|ja|倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)}} (Higashi-Kaminomiya-cho, {{ill|Isesaki, Gunma|ja|伊勢崎市}}, [[Gunma Prefecture]]) * '''Shizuka Shrine (静神社)''' in '''Kuji District, Hitachi Province''' – {{ill|Shizuka Shrine|ja|静神社}} ({{ill|Naka, Ibaraki|ja|那珂市}}, [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Koma District, Kai Province''' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)|ja|倭文神社 (韮崎市)}} (Miyakubo, {{ill|Nirasaki, Yamanashi|ja|韮崎市}}, [[Yamanashi Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Fuji District, Suruga Province''' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)|ja|倭文神社 (富士宮市)}} (Hoshiyama, {{ill|Fujinomiya, Shizuoka|ja|富士宮市}}, [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Tagata District, Izu Province''' – Identified with multiple candidate shrines, including '''{{ill|Kuwado Shrine|ja|鍬戸神社}}''' ({{ill|Mishima, Shizuoka|ja|三島市}}, [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Suzuka District, Ise Province''' – '''{{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社}}''' (merged into [[Suzuka, Mie|鈴鹿市]], [[Mie Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Kasa District, Tango Province''' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Maizuru)|ja|倭文神社 (舞鶴市)}} (Imada, {{ill|Maizuru, Kyoto|ja|舞鶴市}}, [[Kyoto Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Yosa District, Tango Province''' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Yosano)|ja|倭文神社 (与謝野町)}} (Mikawachi, {{ill|Yosano, Kyoto|ja|与謝野町}}, [[Kyoto Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Asago District, Tajima Province''' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Asago)|ja|倭文神社 (朝来市)}} (Maruyama, {{ill|Asago, Hyogo|ja|朝来市}}, [[Hyogo Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Takakusa District, Inaba Province''' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tottori)|ja|倭文神社 (鳥取市)}} (Shidori, {{ill|Tottori, Tottori|ja|鳥取市}}, [[Tottori Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Kawamura District, Hōki Province''' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Yurihama)|ja|倭文神社 (湯梨浜町)}} (Miyauchi, {{ill|Yurihama, Tottori|ja|湯梨浜町}}, [[Tottori Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Kume District, Hōki Province''' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Kurayoshi)|ja|倭文神社 (倉吉市)}} (Shizu, {{ill|Kurayoshi, Tottori|ja|倉吉市}}, [[Tottori Prefecture]])
Other notable Shidori Shrines include:
* '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tono)|ja|倭文神社 (遠野市)}}''' (Tsuchibuchi-cho, {{ill|Tono, Iwate|ja|遠野市}}, [[Iwate Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nara)|ja|倭文神社 (奈良市)}}''' (Nishikujo-cho, {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Kume District, Mimasaka Province''' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|ja|倭文神社 (津山市油木北)}} (Yukikita, {{ill|Tsuyama, Okayama|ja|津山市}}, [[Okayama Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Mihara District, Awaji Province''' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|ja|倭文神社 (あわじ市倭文委文)}} (Shidori, {{ill|Minamiawaji, Hyogo|ja|南あわじ市}}, [[Hyogo Prefecture]])
Additionally, '''{{ill|Oomiwa Shrine|ja|大甕神社}}''' ({{ill|Hitachi, Ibaraki|ja|日立市}}, [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) also enshrines '''Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto'''. Similarly, '''{{ill|Ishii Shrine|ja|石井神社}}''' ({{ill|Kasama, Ibaraki|ja|笠間市}}, [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) enshrines the same deity, with a large sacred rock called "Mitarai" located to the south.
== Related Topics ==
* '''{{ill|Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto}}''' (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神}}''' (also known as Amanoorihime)

[[Category:Imported Shinto cults]] [[Category:kk]][[ja:倭文神社]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Shinto shrines by tradition]] [[Category:Imported Shinto cults]] [[Category:kk]][[ja:倭文神社]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Shizuri Shrines]] [[Category:倭文神社]][[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]]{{Shizuri Faith}}[[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[ja:倭文神社|しすりしんしや]] [[Category:Shizuri Shrines forks]]

''Shidori Shrine'' (倭文神社) refers to Shinto shrines bearing the name "Shidori" (also read as Shizuri, Shitori, or Shidori).
These shrines enshrine ''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto}}'' (also known as ''Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto'', ''Ameno Hachio'', or ''Takehatsuchi'') as the ''deity of weaving'', alongside ''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神}}'' (also known as ''Amano Yachichihime'' or ''Amanoorihime''). These shrines were established by the ''{{ill|Shidori clan|ja|倭文氏}}'', who worshipped Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto as their ancestral deity.
The ''origin of these shrines is attributed to'' the ''{{ill|Katsuraki Shitori ni Imasu Ame no Ha Ikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社}}'' in ''Katsuragi, Nara Prefecture''. However, while the introduction and promotion of silk weaving technology in Japan is credited to ''{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}'', some Shidori Shrines claim their origins date back to the reign of ''{{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}''. Therefore, these shrines provide ''important historical evidence'' regarding ''when and where silk weaving developed in Japan (Wa).''
Additionally, in the ''2016 animated film "Your Name"'', the fictional ''{{ill|Miyamizu Shrine|ja|宮水神社}}'' is depicted as enshrining ''Shidori deity Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto''.
== Major Shidori Shrines ==
The following shrines are listed in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}'':
* ''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)'' in ''Shidori-go, Nanba District, Kōzuke Province'' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)|ja|倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)}} (Higashi-Kaminomiya-cho, {{ill|Isesaki, Gunma|ja|伊勢崎市}}, [[Gunma Prefecture]]) * ''Shizuka Shrine (静神社)'' in ''Kuji District, Hitachi Province'' – {{ill|Shizuka Shrine|ja|静神社}} ({{ill|Naka, Ibaraki|ja|那珂市}}, [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) * ''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)'' in ''Koma District, Kai Province'' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)|ja|倭文神社 (韮崎市)}} (Miyakubo, {{ill|Nirasaki, Yamanashi|ja|韮崎市}}, [[Yamanashi Prefecture]]) * ''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)'' in ''Fuji Additionally, ''{{ill|Oomiwa Shrine|ja|大甕神社}}'' ({{ill|Hitachi, Ibaraki|ja|日立市}}, [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) also enshrines ''Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto''. Other notable Shidori Shrines include:

Additionally, ''{{ill|Ōmika Shrine|8=ja|9=大甕神社|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] <!--Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)-->[[ja:大甕神社]]'' ({{ill|Hitachi, Ibaraki|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] <!--Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)-->[[ja:日立市]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) also enshrines ''Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto''. Similarly, ''{{ill|Ishii Shrine|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] <!--Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)-->[[ja:石井神社]]'' ({{ill|Kasama, Ibaraki|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] <!--Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)-->[[ja:笠間市]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) enshrines the same deity, with a large sacred rock called "Mitarai" located to the south.

Additionally, ''{{ill|Ōmika Shrine|8=ja|9=大甕神社|en}}[[Category:Pages to be written]] [[Category:Pages to be written]] <!--Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)-->[[ja:大甕神社]]'' ({{ill|Hitachi, Ibaraki|en}}[[Category:Pages to be written]] [[Category:Pages to be written]] <!--Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)-->[[ja:日立市]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) also enshrines ''Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto''. Similarly, ''{{ill|Ishii Shrine|en}}[[Category:Pages to be written]] [[Category:Pages to be written]] <!--Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)-->[[ja:石井神社]]'' ({{ill|Kasama, Ibaraki|en}}[[Category:Pages to be written]] [[Category:Pages to be written]] <!--Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)-->[[ja:笠間市]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) enshrines the same deity, with a large sacred rock called "Mitarai" located to the south.
== See Also ==
* ''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|en}}[[Category:Pages to be written]] [[Category:Pages to be written]] <!--Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)-->[[ja:天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto]]'' (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * ''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|en}}[[Category:Pages to be written]] [[Category:Pages to be written]] <!--Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)-->[[ja:天棚機姫神]]'' (also known as Amanoorihime)

revision.text:
{{Shinto shrine | name = '''Shidori Jinja''' (倭文神社) | image = [[File:Shidori Shrine.JPG|250px]]
Haiden and torii | location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|de|Shizuoka Präfektur|ja|静岡県|zh|静冈县}}{{ill|Fujinomiya|de|Fujinomiya|ja|富士宮市|zh|富士宫市}} Hoshiyama 1 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|12|11.6|N|138|36|39.9|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | enshrinedDeity = | shrineRank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|zh|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha (small shrine)|lt_ja=式内小社|lt_de=Shikinaisha (small shrine)|lt_zh=Shikinaisha (small shrine)}}、former {{ill|village shrine|de|Dorfschrein|ja|村社|zh|村社}} | founded = Unknown | annualFestival = | map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=倭文神社|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|12|11.6|N|138|36|39.9|E}}}} }} '''Shidori Jinja''' (倭文神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|de|Shinto Schrein|ja|神社|zh|神社}} located in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|de|Shizuoka Präfektur|ja|静岡県|zh|静冈县}}, {{ill|Fujinomiya|de|Fujinomiya|ja|富士宮市|zh|富士宫市}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|zh|Shikinaisha}} and its former {{ill|shrine rank|de|Schreinrang|ja|社格|zh|神社等级}} was that of a {{ill|village shrine|de|Dorfschrein|ja|村社|zh|村社}}. ==History== In the '''{{ill|Engishiki|de|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|zh|Engishiki}}''' (Vol. 9, Shingi 9), Shidori Jinja is recorded as one of the '''{{ill|Three shrines of Fuji District|de|Drei Schreine des Fuji-Distrikts|ja|富士郡|zh|富士郡}}三座''' and is identified as the same shrine. Also, in the same ''Engishiki'' (Vol. 24, Shukei section), “thirty-one bolts of woven textile (倭文卅一端)” are listed among Suruga Province’s tribute items, suggesting that the local 倭文部 (Shitori-be) lived in this area. In the Middle Ages, the local Suruga power '''{{ill|Fuji clan|de|Fuji Klan|ja|富士氏|zh|富士氏}}''' is said to have worshipped here. 岡田米夫、『日本史小百科 神社』、近藤出版、1977年、146頁 ==Notes== {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} ==References== * Nito Atsushi, "Suruga Kuni no Miyatsuko and Suruga Province," in ''Susono City History Research'' No.4, 1992 * Nito Atsushi, "Ancient Clans around the Suruga District," in ''Susono City History Research'' No.10, 1998 {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suruga Province]] [[Category:History of Fujinomiya]] [[Category:Buildings in Fujinomiya]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=]] [[Category:Pages to be translated]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]][[sn:Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith new pages]] [[ja:%E5%80%AD%E6%96%87%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%AF%8C%E5%A3%AB%E5%AE%AE%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:&action=edit]] {{Shizuri Faith}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[ja:倭文神社]] [[de:Shidori Jinja]]

{{infobox religious building | name = '''Shidori Jinja''' (倭文神社) | image = [[File:Shidori Shrine.JPG|250px]]
Haiden and torii | location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|de|Shizuoka Präfektur|ja|静岡県|zh|静冈县}}{{ill|Fujinomiya|de|Fujinomiya|ja|富士宮市|zh|富士宫市}} Hoshiyama 1 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|12|11.6|N|138|36|39.9|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | enshrinedDeity = | shrineRank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|zh|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha (small shrine)|lt_ja=式内小社|lt_de=Shikinaisha (small shrine)|lt_zh=Shikinaisha (small shrine)}}、former {{ill|village shrine|de|Dorfschrein|ja|村社|zh|村社}} | founded = Unknown | annualFestival = | map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=倭文神社|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|12|11.6|N|138|36|39.9|E}}}} }} '''Shidori Jinja''' (倭文神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|de|Shinto Schrein|ja|神社|zh|神社}} located in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|de|Shizuoka Präfektur|ja|静岡県|zh|静冈县}}, {{ill|Fujinomiya|de|Fujinomiya|ja|富士宮市|zh|富士宫市}}. In the Middle Ages, the local Suruga power '''{{ill|Fuji clan|de|Fuji Klan|ja|富士氏|zh|富士氏}}''' is said to have worshipped here. 岡田米夫、『日本史小百科 神社』、近藤出版、1977年、146頁 ==Notes== {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} ==References== * Nito Atsushi, "Suruga Kuni no Miyatsuko and Suruga Province," in ''Susono City History Research'' No.4, 1992 * Nito Atsushi, "Ancient Clans around the Suruga District," in ''Susono City History Research'' No.10, 1998 {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suruga Province]] [[Category:History of Fujinomiya]] [[Category:Buildings in Fujinomiya]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=]] [[Category:Pages to be translated]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]][[sn:Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith new pages]] [[ja:%E5%80%AD%E6%96%87%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%AF%8C%E5%A3%AB%E5%AE%AE%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:&action=edit]] {{Shizuri Faith}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[ja:倭文神社]] [[de:Shidori Jinja]]

It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|zh|Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and its former {{ill|shrine Rank|de|Schreinrang|ja|社格|zh|神社等级|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine Rank|qq=}} was that of a {{ill|village shrine|de|Dorfschrein|ja|村社|zh|村社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=}}. ==History== In the '''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]''' (Vol. 9, Shingi 9), Shidori Jinja is recorded as one of the '''[[:en:Fuji District, Shizuoka|Three shrines of Fuji District]]''' and is identified as the same shrine. Also, in the same ''Engishiki'' (Vol. 24, Shukei section), “thirty-one bolts of woven textile (倭文卅一端)” are listed among Suruga Province’s tribute items, suggesting that the local 倭文部 (Shitori-be) lived in this area. In the Middle Ages, the local Suruga power '''{{ill|Fuji clan|de|Fuji Klan|ja|富士氏|zh|富士氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji clan|qq=}}''' is said to have worshipped here. 岡田米夫、『日本史小百科 神社』、近藤出版、1977年、146頁 ==Notes== {{Reflist}} ==References== * Nito Atsushi, "Suruga Kuni no Miyatsuko and Suruga Province," in ''Susono City History Research'' No.4, 1992 * Nito Atsushi, "Ancient Clans around the Suruga District," in ''Susono City History Research'' No.10, 1998 {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{translated page|ja|倭文神社 (富士宮市)|version=101989934|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suruga Province]] [[Category:History of Fujinomiya]] [[Category:Buildings in Fujinomiya]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]][[sn:Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)]][[Category:Shizuri Faith]] }} [[sn:Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)]] [[ja:%E5%80%AD%E6%96%87%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%AF%8C%E5%A3%AB%E5%AE%AE%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:&action=edit]] [[ja:倭文神社]] [[de:Shidori Jinja]] [[Category:富士宮市の建築物]] [[Category:富士宮市の歴史]] [[ja:倭文神社 (富士宮市)]]

In the Middle Ages, the local Suruga power '''{{ill|Fuji clan|de|Fuji Klan|ja|富士氏|zh|富士氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji clan|qq=}}''' is said to have worshipped here. 岡田米夫、『日本史小百科 神社』、近藤出版、1977年、146頁 ==Notes== {{Reflist}} ==References== * Nito Atsushi, "Suruga Kuni no Miyatsuko and Suruga Province," in ''Susono City History Research'' No.4, 1992 * Nito Atsushi, "Ancient Clans around the Suruga District," in ''Susono City History Research'' No.10, 1998 {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{translated page|ja|倭文神社 (富士宮市)|version=101989934|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suruga Province]] [[Category:History of Fujinomiya]] [[Category:Buildings in Fujinomiya]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]][[sn:Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)]][[Category:Shizuri Faith]] }} [[sn:Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)]] [[ja:%E5%80%AD%E6%96%87%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%AF%8C%E5%A3%AB%E5%AE%AE%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:&action=edit]] [[ja:倭文神社]] [[de:Shidori Jinja]] [[Category:Buildings in Fujinomiya]] [[Category:History of Fujinomiya]] [[ja:倭文神社 (富士宮市)]]

It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|zh|Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and its former {{ill|shrine Rank|de|Schreinrang|ja|社格|zh|神社等级|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine Rank|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} was that of a {{ill|village shrine|de|Dorfschrein|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|zh|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. ==History== In the '''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]''' (Vol. 9, Shingi 9), Shidori Jinja is recorded as one of the '''[[:en:Fuji District, Shizuoka|Three shrines of Fuji District]]''' and is identified as the same shrine. Also, in the same ''Engishiki'' (Vol. 24, Shukei section), “thirty-one bolts of woven textile (倭文卅一端)” are listed among Suruga Province’s tribute items, suggesting that the local 倭文部 (Shitori-be) lived in this area. In the Middle Ages, the local Suruga power '''{{ill|Fuji clan|de|Fuji Klan|ja|富士氏|zh|富士氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji clan|qq=}}''' is said to have worshipped here. 岡田米夫、『日本史小百科 神社』、近藤出版、1977年、146頁 ==Notes== {{Reflist}} ==References== * Nito Atsushi, "Suruga Kuni no Miyatsuko and Suruga Province," in ''Susono City History Research'' No.4, 1992 * Nito Atsushi, "Ancient Clans around the Suruga District," in ''Susono City History Research'' No.10, 1998 {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{translated page|ja|倭文神社 (富士宮市)|version=101989934|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suruga Province]] [[Category:History of Fujinomiya]] [[Category:Buildings in Fujinomiya]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]][[sn:Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)]][[Category:Shizuri Faith]] }} [[sn:Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)]] [[sn:E5%80%AD%E6%96%87%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%AF%8C%E5%A3%AB%E5%AE%AE%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:&action=edit]] [[ja:倭文神社]] [[de:Shidori Jinja]] [[Category:Buildings in Fujinomiya]] [[Category:History of Fujinomiya]] [[ja:倭文神社 (富士宮市)]] [[Category:paes with no wikidata]]

It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|zh|Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and its former {{ill|shrine Rank|de|Schreinrang|ja|社格|zh|神社等级|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine Rank|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}} was that of a {{ill|village shrine|de|Dorfschrein|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|zh|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. ==History== In the '''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]''' (Vol. 9, Shingi 9), Shidori Jinja is recorded as one of the '''[[:en:Fuji District, Shizuoka|Three shrines of Fuji District]]''' and is identified as the same shrine. Also, in the same ''Engishiki'' (Vol. 24, Shukei section), “thirty-one bolts of woven textile (倭文卅一端)” are listed among Suruga Province’s tribute items, suggesting that the local 倭文部 (Shitori-be) lived in this area. In the Middle Ages, the local Suruga power '''{{ill|Fuji clan|de|Fuji Klan|ja|富士氏|zh|富士氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji clan|qq=|1=Fuji clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11456091}}''' is said to have worshipped here. 岡田米夫、『日本史小百科 神社』、近藤出版、1977年、146頁 ==Notes== {{Reflist}} ==References== * Nito Atsushi, "Suruga Kuni no Miyatsuko and Suruga Province," in ''Susono City History Research'' No.4, 1992 * Nito Atsushi, "Ancient Clans around the Suruga District," in ''Susono City History Research'' No.10, 1998 {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{translated page|ja|倭文神社 (富士宮市)|version=101989934|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suruga Province]] [[Category:History of Fujinomiya]] [[Category:Buildings in Fujinomiya]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]][[sn:Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)]][[Category:Shizuri Faith]] }} [[sn:Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)]] [[sn:E5%80%AD%E6%96%87%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%AF%8C%E5%A3%AB%E5%AE%AE%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:倭文神社]] [[de:Shidori Jinja]] [[Category:Buildings in Fujinomiya]] [[Category:History of Fujinomiya]] [[en:Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)]] [[ja:倭文神社 (富士宮市)]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|zh|Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and its former {{ill|shrine Rank|de|Schreinrang|ja|社格|zh|神社等级|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}} was that of a {{ill|village shrine|de|Dorfschrein|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|zh|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. ==History== In the '''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]''' (Vol. 9, Shingi 9), Shidori Jinja is recorded as one of the '''[[:en:Fuji District, Shizuoka|Three shrines of Fuji District]]''' and is identified as the same shrine. Also, in the same ''Engishiki'' (Vol. 24, Shukei section), “thirty-one bolts of woven textile (倭文卅一端)” are listed among Suruga Province’s tribute items, suggesting that the local 倭文部 (Shitori-be) lived in this area. In the Middle Ages, the local Suruga power '''{{ill|Fuji clan|de|Fuji Klan|ja|富士氏|zh|富士氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji clan|1=Fuji clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11456091}}''' is said to have worshipped here. 岡田米夫、『日本史小百科 神社』、近藤出版、1977年、146頁 ==Notes== {{Reflist}} ==References== * Nito Atsushi, "Suruga Kuni no Miyatsuko and Suruga Province," in ''Susono City History Research'' No.4, 1992 * Nito Atsushi, "Ancient Clans around the Suruga District," in ''Susono City History Research'' No.10, 1998 {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{translated page|ja|倭文神社 (富士宮市)|version=101989934|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suruga Province]] [[Category:History of Fujinomiya]] [[Category:Buildings in Fujinomiya]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]][[Category:Shizuri Faith]] }} [[ja:倭文神社]] [[de:Shidori Jinja]] [[Category:Buildings in Fujinomiya]] [[Category:History of Fujinomiya]] [[ja:倭文神社 (富士宮市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11387089}}

It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|zh|Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and its former {{ill|shrine Rank|de|Schreinrang|ja|社格|zh|神社等级|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}} was that of a {{ill|village shrine|de|Dorfschrein|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|zh|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. ==History== In the '''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]''' (Vol. 9, Shingi 9), Shidori Jinja is recorded as one of the '''[[:en:Fuji District, Shizuoka|Three shrines of Fuji District]]''' and is identified as the same shrine. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|zh|Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and its former {{ill|shrine Rank|de|Schreinrang|ja|社格|zh|神社等级|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}} was that of a {{ill|village shrine|de|Dorfschrein|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|zh|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. ==History== In the '''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]''' (Vol. 9, Shingi 9), Shidori Jinja is recorded as one of the '''[[:en:Fuji District, Shizuoka|Three shrines of Fuji District]]''' and is identified as the same shrine. Also, in the same ''Engishiki'' (Vol. 24, Shukei section), “thirty-one bolts of woven textile (倭文卅一端)” are listed among Suruga Province’s tribute items, suggesting that the local 倭文部 (Shitori-be) lived in this area. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|zh|Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and its former {{ill|shrine Rank|de|Schreinrang|ja|社格|zh|神社等级|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}} was that of a {{ill|village shrine|de|Dorfschrein|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|zh|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. ==History== In the '''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]''' (Vol. 9, Shingi 9), Shidori Jinja is recorded as one of the '''[[:en:Fuji District, Shizuoka|Three shrines of Fuji District]]''' and is identified as the same shrine. Also, in the same ''Engishiki'' (Vol. 24, Shukei section), “thirty-one bolts of woven textile (倭文卅一端)” are listed among Suruga Province’s tribute items, suggesting that the local 倭文部 (Shitori-be) lived in this area. In the Middle Ages, the local Suruga power '''{{ill|Fuji clan|de|Fuji Klan|ja|富士氏|zh|富士氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji clan|1=Fuji clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11456091}}''' is said to have worshipped here. 岡田米夫、『日本史小百科 神社』、近藤出版、1977年、146頁 ==Notes== {{Reflist}} ==References== * Nito Atsushi, "Suruga Kuni no Miyatsuko and Suruga Province," in ''Susono City History Research'' No.4, 1992 * Nito Atsushi, "Ancient Clans around the Suruga District," in ''Susono City History Research'' No.10, 1998 {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{translated page|ja|倭文神社 (富士宮市)|version=101989934|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suruga Province]] [[Category:History of Fujinomiya]] [[Category:Buildings in Fujinomiya]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]][[Category:Shizuri Faith]] }} [[ja:倭文神社]] [[de:Shidori Jinja]] [[Category:Buildings in Fujinomiya]] [[Category:History of Fujinomiya]] [[ja:倭文神社 (富士宮市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11387089}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}}

It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|zh|Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and its former {{ill|shrine Rank|de|Schreinrang|ja|社格|zh|神社等级|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}} was that of a {{ill|village shrine|de|Dorfschrein|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|zh|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. ==History== In the '''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]''' (Vol. 9, Shingi 9), Shidori Jinja is recorded as one of the '''[[:en:Fuji District, Shizuoka|Three shrines of Fuji District]]''' and is identified as the same shrine. Also, in the same ''Engishiki'' (Vol. 24, Shukei section), “thirty-one bolts of woven textile (倭文卅一端)” are listed among Suruga Province’s tribute items, suggesting that the local 倭文部 (Shitori-be) lived in this area. In the Middle Ages, the local Suruga power '''{{ill|Fuji clan|de|Fuji Klan|ja|富士氏|zh|富士氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji clan|1=Fuji clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11456091}}''' is said to have worshipped here. 岡田米夫、『日本史小百科 神社』、近藤出版、1977年、146頁 ==Notes== {{Reflist}} ==References== * Nito Atsushi, "Suruga Kuni no Miyatsuko and Suruga Province," in ''Susono City History Research'' No.4, 1992 * Nito Atsushi, "Ancient Clans around the Suruga District," in ''Susono City History Research'' No.10, 1998 {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{translated page|ja|倭文神社 (富士宮市)|version=101989934|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:倭文神社]] [[de:Shidori Jinja]] [[ja:倭文神社 (富士宮市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11387089}} [[Category:Buildings in Fujinomiya]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Fujinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suruga Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Haiden and torii | location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|Fujinomiya, Shizuoka|en|Fujinomiya, Shizuoka|WD=Q496072|lt=Fujinomiya}} Hoshiyama 1 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|12|11.6|N|138|36|39.9|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | enshrinedDeity = | shrineRank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|zh|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha (small shrine)|lt_ja=式内小社|lt_de=Shikinaisha (small shrine)|lt_zh=Shikinaisha (small shrine)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}、former {{ill|village shrine|de|Dorfschrein|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|zh|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | founded = Unknown | annualFestival = | map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=倭文神社|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|12|11.6|N|138|36|39.9|E}}}} }} '''Shidori Jinja''' (倭文神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Fujinomiya, Shizuoka|en|Fujinomiya, Shizuoka|WD=Q496072|lt=Fujinomiya}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|zh|Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and its former {{ill|shrine Rank|de|Schreinrang|ja|社格|zh|神社等级|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}} was that of a {{ill|village shrine|de|Dorfschrein|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|zh|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. ==History== In the '''{{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}}''' (Vol. 9, Shingi 9), Shidori Jinja is recorded as one of the '''{{ill|Fuji District, Shizuoka|en|Fuji District, Shizuoka|WD=Q1154962|lt=Three shrines of Fuji District}}''' and is identified as the same shrine. Also, in the same ''Engishiki'' (Vol. 24, Shukei section), “thirty-one bolts of woven textile (倭文卅一端)” are listed among Suruga Province’s tribute items, suggesting that the local 倭文部 (Shitori-be) lived in this area. In the Middle Ages, the local Suruga power '''{{ill|Fuji clan|de|Fuji Klan|ja|富士氏|zh|富士氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji clan|1=Fuji clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11456091}}''' is said to have worshipped here. 岡田米夫、『日本史小百科 神社』、近藤出版、1977年、146頁 ==Notes== {{Reflist}} ==References== * Nito Atsushi, "Suruga Kuni no Miyatsuko and Suruga Province," in ''Susono City History Research'' No.4, 1992 * Nito Atsushi, "Ancient Clans around the Suruga District," in ''Susono City History Research'' No.10, 1998 {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{translated page|ja|倭文神社 (富士宮市)|version=101989934|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:倭文神社]] [[de:Shidori Jinja]] [[ja:倭文神社 (富士宮市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11387089}} [[Category:Buildings in Fujinomiya]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Fujinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suruga Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[ja:倭文神社 (富士宮市)]] [[ja:倭文神社 (富士宮市)]] [[ja:倭文神社 (富士宮市)]] {{infobox religious building | name = '''Shidori Jinja''' (倭文神社) | image = [[File:Shidori Shrine.JPG|250px]]
Haiden and torii | location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|Fujinomiya, Shizuoka|en|Fujinomiya, Shizuoka|WD=Q496072|lt=Fujinomiya}} Hoshiyama 1 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|12|11.6|N|138|36|39.9|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | enshrinedDeity = | shrineRank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|zh|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha (small shrine)|lt_ja=式内小社|lt_de=Shikinaisha (small shrine)|lt_zh=Shikinaisha (small shrine)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}、former {{ill|village shrine|de|Dorfschrein|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|zh|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | founded = Unknown | annualFestival = | map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=倭文神社|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|12|11.6|N|138|36|39.9|E}}}} }} '''Shidori Jinja''' (倭文神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Fujinomiya, Shizuoka|en|Fujinomiya, Shizuoka|WD=Q496072|lt=Fujinomiya}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|zh|Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and its former {{ill|shrine Rank|de|Schreinrang|ja|社格|zh|神社等级|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}} was that of a {{ill|village shrine|de|Dorfschrein|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|zh|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. ==History== In the '''{{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}}''' (Vol. 9, Shingi 9), Shidori Jinja is recorded as one of the '''{{ill|Fuji District, Shizuoka|en|Fuji District, Shizuoka|WD=Q1154962|lt=Three shrines of Fuji District}}''' and is identified as the same shrine. Also, in the same ''Engishiki'' (Vol. 24, Shukei section), “thirty-one bolts of woven textile (倭文卅一端)” are listed among Suruga Province’s tribute items, suggesting that the local 倭文部 (Shitori-be) lived in this area. In the Middle Ages, the local Suruga power '''{{ill|Fuji clan|de|Fuji Klan|ja|富士氏|zh|富士氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji clan|1=Fuji clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11456091}}''' is said to have worshipped here. 岡田米夫、『日本史小百科 神社』、近藤出版、1977年、146頁 ==Notes== {{Reflist}} ==References== * Nito Atsushi, "Suruga Kuni no Miyatsuko and Suruga Province," in ''Susono City History Research'' No.4, 1992 * Nito Atsushi, "Ancient Clans around the Suruga District," in ''Susono City History Research'' No.10, 1998 {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{translated page|ja|倭文神社 (富士宮市)|version=101989934|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:倭文神社]] [[de:Shidori Jinja]] [[ja:倭文神社 (富士宮市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11387089}} [[Category:Buildings in Fujinomiya]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Fujinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suruga Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]

[[ja:倭文神社 (富士宮市)]] [[ja:倭文神社 (富士宮市)]] [[ja:倭文神社 (富士宮市)]] {{infobox religious building | name = '''Shidori Jinja''' (倭文神社) | image = [[File:Shidori Shrine.JPG|250px]]
Haiden and torii | location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|Fujinomiya, Shizuoka|en|Fujinomiya, Shizuoka|WD=Q496072|lt=Fujinomiya|1=Fujinomiya, Shizuoka}} Hoshiyama 1 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|12|11.6|N|138|36|39.9|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | enshrinedDeity = | shrineRank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|zh|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha (small shrine)|lt_ja=式内小社|lt_de=Shikinaisha (small shrine)|lt_zh=Shikinaisha (small shrine)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}、former {{ill|village shrine|de|Dorfschrein|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|zh|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | founded = Unknown | annualFestival = | map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=倭文神社|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|12|11.6|N|138|36|39.9|E}}}} }} '''Shidori Jinja''' (倭文神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Fujinomiya, Shizuoka|en|Fujinomiya, Shizuoka|WD=Q496072|lt=Fujinomiya|1=Fujinomiya, Shizuoka}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|zh|Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and its former {{ill|shrine Rank|de|Schreinrang|ja|社格|zh|神社等级|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shakaku|WD=Q10444029}} was that of a {{ill|village shrine|de|Dorfschrein|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|zh|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. ==History== In the '''{{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}''' (Vol. 9, Shingi 9), Shidori Jinja is recorded as one of the '''{{ill|Fuji District, Shizuoka|en|Fuji District, Shizuoka|WD=Q1154962|lt=Three shrines of Fuji District|1=Fuji District, Shizuoka}}''' and is identified as the same shrine. Also, in the same ''Engishiki'' (Vol. 24, Shukei section), “thirty-one bolts of woven textile (倭文卅一端)” are listed among Suruga Province’s tribute items, suggesting that the local 倭文部 (Shitori-be) lived in this area. In the Middle Ages, the local Suruga power '''{{ill|Fuji clan|de|Fuji Klan|ja|富士氏|zh|富士氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji clan|1=Fuji clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11456091}}''' is said to have worshipped here. 岡田米夫、『日本史小百科 神社』、近藤出版、1977年、146頁 ==Notes== {{Reflist}} ==References== * Nito Atsushi, "Suruga Kuni no Miyatsuko and Suruga Province," in ''Susono City History Research'' No.4, 1992 * Nito Atsushi, "Ancient Clans around the Suruga District," in ''Susono City History Research'' No.10, 1998 {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{translated page|ja|倭文神社 (富士宮市)|version=101989934|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:倭文神社]] [[de:Shidori Jinja]] [[ja:倭文神社 (富士宮市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11387089}} [[Category:Buildings in Fujinomiya]] [[Category:History of Fujinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suruga Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]
